Sem1_CSE111.name: ORIENTATION TO COMPUTING-I
Sem1_CSE111.desc: This course focuses on skill development and employability by introducing the functional components of computer systems, operating systems like Linux, and networking basics. It also emphasizes the utilization of professional tools such as Git, GitHub, and AI platforms to enhance technical skills and digital practices.

Sem1_CSE326.name: INTERNET PROGRAMMING LABORATORY
Sem1_CSE326.desc: This course focuses on internet programming techniques including HTML5 for structure, CSS for responsive design and layout, and JavaScript for client-side logic and DOM manipulation.

Sem1_INT108.name: PYTHON PROGRAMMING
Sem1_INT108.desc: This course focuses on employability and skill development through Python programming. It covers the installation of the Python environment, conditional and iterative statements, functions, core data structures like lists, dictionaries, tuples and sets, object-oriented programming, and file handling operations.
units: Setting up Programming Environment; Variables, Expressions and Statements

Sem1_MTH165.name: MATHEMATICS FOR ENGINEERS
Sem1_MTH165.desc: This course focuses on employability and skill development, enabling students to apply concepts of matrices, calculus of one variable, multi-variable differential and integral calculus, and Fourier series to engineering problems.

Sem1_ECE131.name: BASIC ELECTRICAL AND ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING
Sem1_ECE131.desc: This course covers the fundamental principles of electrical and electronics engineering, including electrical circuits, machines, semiconductor devices, and operational amplifiers. It also introduces concepts of IoT, cloud computing, and embedded systems.
units: Fundamentals of Semiconductor Devices and Digital Circuits
Fundamentals of Filters and Operational Amplifiers
Fundamentals of Embedded System and Applications

Sem2_CSE101.name: COMPUTER PROGRAMMING
Sem2_CSE101.desc: Course Focus: EMPLOYABILITY, SKILL DEVELOPMENT, ENTREPRENEURSHIP. Covers C programming fundamentals including control structures, functions, arrays, pointers, dynamic memory allocation, strings, structures and unions.
units: User Defined Functions and Storage Classes
Pointers, Dynamic Memory Allocation and Strings
Derived Types Including Structures and Unions

Sem2_MTH401.name: DISCRETE MATHEMATICS
Sem2_MTH401.desc: This course on Discrete Mathematics focuses on employability and skill development, covering topics such as logic and proofs, combinatorics, recurrence relations, graph theory, and number theory.
units: Number Theory and its Application in Cryptography

Sem2_PEL130.name: ADVANCED COMMUNICATION SKILLS I
Sem2_PEL130.desc: The course focuses on competitive and placement examinations for higher education and mass recruiters, along with skill enhancement and personality development.
units: Reduced Clauses, Connectors and Focus Structures

Sem2_ECE182.name: ELECTRONICS FOR ROBOTS
Sem2_ECE182.desc: This course focuses on employability and skill development, enabling students to understand, analyze, and apply electronic devices such as p-n junction diodes, BJTs, FETs, and op-amps.

Sem2_ECE213.name: DIGITAL ELECTRONICS
Sem2_ECE213.desc: This course covers digital number systems, boolean algebra, and the application of binary arithmetic concepts for designing combinational circuits. Students will also learn to develop various flip-flops, registers, counters, and analyze memory design and FPGA applications.

Sem3_CSE202.name: OBJECT ORIENTED PROGRAMMING
Sem3_CSE202.desc: The course focuses on employability, skill development, and entrepreneurship, enabling students to identify basic programming constructs, interpret object-oriented model principles, and apply them in C++ language implementation.
units: Concepts and Basics of C++ Programming and Functions
Pointers, Reference Variables, Arrays and String Concepts
Data File Operations, Constructors, Destructors and File Handling
Operator Overloading, Type Conversion and Inheritance

Sem3_CSE205.name: DATA STRUCTURES AND ALGORITHMS
Sem3_CSE205.desc: This course focuses on employability and skill development, designed to enable students to understand time and space complexity of programs and data structures. It covers the implementation and importance of Linked Lists, Stacks, Queues, Trees, Heap operations, and Hashing techniques.

Sem3_CSE306.name: COMPUTER NETWORKS
Sem3_CSE306.desc: This course covers the fundamentals of computer networking, including network models, protocols, data transmission, routing algorithms, and network security concepts.

Sem3_CSE316.name: OPERATING SYSTEMS
Sem3_CSE316.desc: This course covers the core concepts of operating systems including process management, memory management, file systems, and I/O management.

Sem3_CSC104.name: IT FUNDAMENTALS
Sem3_CSC104.desc: This course covers the fundamental concepts of Information Technology including hardware, software, networking, databases, and security.

Sem4_CSE211.name: COMPUTER ORGANIZATION AND DESIGN
Sem4_CSE211.desc: This course covers the fundamentals of computer organization including digital logic, processor design, memory hierarchy, and I/O systems.

Sem4_CSE273.name: CSE273
Sem4_CSE273.desc: Core computer science course covering advanced topics in algorithms and system design.

Sem4_CSE310.name: PROGRAMMING IN JAVA
Sem4_CSE310.desc: This course covers Java programming fundamentals including OOP concepts, exception handling, multithreading, collections framework, and GUI development.

Sem4_CSE325.name: OPERATING SYSTEMS LABORATORY
Sem4_CSE325.desc: Practical laboratory course focusing on hands-on experience with operating system concepts including process management, scheduling, and memory management.

Sem4_INT428.name: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE ESSENTIALS
Sem4_INT428.desc: This course covers the foundations and applications of AI, including problem solving, search algorithms, machine learning, deep neural networks, NLP, generative AI, and prompt engineering.
units: Introduction to Deep Neural Networks / Modern NLP

Sem4_PEA305.name: ANALYTICAL SKILLS-I
Sem4_PEA305.desc: This course focuses on developing analytical and quantitative aptitude skills for competitive exams and placements.

Sem4_CSE274.name: APPLIED MACHINE LEARNING
Sem4_CSE274.desc: This course covers practical machine learning techniques including supervised learning, unsupervised learning, model evaluation, and deployment.

Sem4_CSE275.name: OPTIMIZATION TECHNIQUES FOR ML
Sem4_CSE275.desc: This course covers mathematical optimization techniques used in machine learning algorithms.

Sem4_INT219.name: FRONT END WEB DEVELOPER
Sem4_INT219.desc: This course covers front-end web development technologies including HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and modern frameworks.

Sem4_INT222.name: ADVANCED WEB DEVELOPMENT
Sem4_INT222.desc: This course covers advanced concepts in web development including server-side programming, databases, APIs, and full-stack development.

Sem4_INT242.name: CYBER SECURITY ESSENTIALS
Sem4_INT242.desc: This course covers fundamental concepts of cybersecurity including threat analysis, cryptography, network security, and security management.

Sem4_INT249.name: SYSTEM ADMINISTRATION
Sem4_INT249.desc: This course covers system administration concepts including server management, user management, storage, and network services.

Sem4_INT255.name: MATHEMATICS BEHIND MACHINE LEARNING
Sem4_INT255.desc: This course covers the mathematical foundations essential for understanding machine learning algorithms.

Sem4_CSC202.name: SYSTEM ADMINISTRATION
Sem4_CSC202.desc: This specialty course covers system administration concepts for managing enterprise IT infrastructure.

Sem4_CSC203.name: INTRODUCTION TO BLOCKCHAIN
Sem4_CSC203.desc: This course covers blockchain technology fundamentals including distributed ledgers, consensus mechanisms, smart contracts, and cryptocurrency.

Sem4_ERT425.name: ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND BUSINESS BASICS
Sem4_ERT425.desc: This course covers the fundamentals of entrepreneurship and business management.

Sem5_CSE322.name: CSE322
Sem5_CSE322.desc: Core computer science course for semester 5.

Sem5_PEV301.name: VERBAL ABILITY
Sem5_PEV301.desc: This course focuses on developing verbal ability skills for competitive exams and placements.

Sem5_INT222.name: ADVANCED WEB DEVELOPMENT
Sem5_INT222.desc: Advanced concepts in web development including server-side programming, databases, APIs, and full-stack development.

Sem5_INT234.desc: This course covers predictive analytics techniques including regression, classification, time series analysis, and model deployment.

Sem5_INT244.name: SECURING COMPUTING SYSTEMS
Sem5_INT244.desc: This course covers advanced security concepts for computing systems.

Sem5_INT250.name: DIGITAL EVIDENCE ANALYSIS
Sem5_INT250.desc: This course covers digital forensics and evidence analysis techniques.

Sem5_INT252.name: WEB APP DEVELOPMENT WITH REACTJS
Sem5_INT252.desc: This course covers modern web application development using ReactJS including components, state management, routing, and deployment.

Sem5_INT344.name: NATURAL LANGUAGE PROCESSING
Sem5_INT344.desc: This course covers NLP techniques including text processing, sentiment analysis, language models, and modern transformer architectures.

Sem5_INT363.name: CLOUD MICROSERVICES
Sem5_INT363.desc: This course covers microservices architecture, containerization, and cloud-native application development.

Sem5_INT364.name: CLOUD ARCHITECTURE AND IMPLEMENTATION-II
Sem5_INT364.desc: Advanced cloud computing concepts including multi-cloud strategies, serverless computing, and cloud security.

Sem5_INT374.name: DATA ANALYTICS WITH POWER BI
Sem5_INT374.desc: This course covers data analytics and visualization using Microsoft Power BI.

Sem5_INT394.name: MACHINE LEARNING ALGORITHMS
Sem5_INT394.desc: This course covers core machine learning algorithms including supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning.

Sem5_INT395.name: SUPERVISED LEARNING
Sem5_INT395.desc: This course covers supervised learning techniques in depth including regression, classification, and ensemble methods.

Sem5_INT423.name: MACHINE LEARNING-II
Sem5_INT423.desc: Advanced machine learning topics including deep learning, reinforcement learning, and generative models.

Sem5_ENG606.name: CONTEMPORARY SHORT STORIES

Sem5_GEO295.desc: This open minor course covers the physical aspects of geography including landforms, climate, and ecosystems.

Sem5_HIS291.name: ANCIENT INDIAN HISTORY AND CULTURE
Sem5_HIS291.desc: This open minor course explores the history and cultural heritage of ancient India.

Sem5_HRM203.name: HUMAN RESOURCE PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT
Sem5_HRM203.desc: This open minor course covers HR planning, recruitment, training, and organizational development.

Sem5_INT323.name: DATABASE ESSENTIALS TOWARD INFORMATICA
Sem5_INT323.desc: This course covers database management concepts with focus on Informatica tools for data integration.

Sem5_INT346.desc: This course covers RPA concepts, tools, and implementation for business process automation.

Sem6_CSE332.desc: This course covers ethical and legal considerations in the IT industry including intellectual property, privacy, and cyber law.
